label: Dynamic sky — rotating god rays + breathing Snell window (Act 2 DESCENSION)
notes: First pass used abs(sin(angle*7)) — created 14-spoke geometric starburst (abs doubles the peaks). Fixed: pow(max(0, sin(angle*5)), 7) gives 5 clean spokes, only positive half-cycles. FBM angle warp (fbm(uv*1.1 + t*0.018)*0.7) bends spokes organically so they read as god rays distorted by moving water surface rather than mechanical rotation. Breathing Snell window: sin(t*1.26)*0.5+0.5 drives snellR 0.56->0.65 on ~5s cycle, core sky brightness breathes ±18%. Both features read Act 2 DESCENSION language: spiraling circles, pull toward center intensifies.

operations.note: Full rewrite — added rotating FBM-warped god rays (5 spokes, slow CW rotation) + breathing Snell window (5s cycle). Replaces static radial with dynamic animated sky.
operations.before: high-contrast radial: exp(-r*3.0) center, cloud fbm*0.18, Snell rim at r=0.62, no animation
